title: Question 1: Data Types (MCQ)
description:
Which of the following data types is most appropriate for storing a single character like ('A')?

A) string
B) char
C) bool
D) int
codeSnippet:
/*
Answer: B
The char type is designed to store exactly one character.
*/

title: Question 2: Comments (MCQ)
description:
What is the correct way to write a single-line comment in C#?

A) /* comment */
B) ``
C) // comment
D) # comment
codeSnippet:
/*
Answer: C
Double forward slashes are used for single-line comments.
*/

title: Question 3: Division Output (MCQ)
description:
What is the output of the following code?

A) 3.33
B) 3
C) 1
D) Error

title: Question 4: Variable Naming (MCQ)
description:
Which of the following variable names (Identifiers) is invalid and will cause an error?

A) player1_score
B) _userAge
C) 1stNumber
D) totalSalary
codeSnippet:
/*
Answer: C
A variable name cannot start with a number.
*/

title: Question 5: Console Input (MCQ)
description:
The Console.ReadLine() method always receives user input as...?

A) Integers (int)
B) Text (string)
C) Decimals (double)
D) Characters (char)
codeSnippet:
/*
Answer: B
It always reads the input as text. If a number is needed, it must be parsed using Parse.
*/

title: Question 6: String Concatenation (MCQ)
description:
To concatenate two strings or variables in a print statement, we use the sign:

A) &
B) *
C) #
D) +
codeSnippet:
/*
Answer: D
The plus sign is used for concatenating strings and variables.
*/

title: Question 7: String to Number Conversion (MCQ)
codeSnippet:
/*
Answer: B
We use int.Parse to convert text into an integer.
*/

title: Question 8: Modulo Operator (MCQ)
description:
What is the result of the following arithmetic operation (Remainder)?

A) 3
B) 4
C) 0
D) 3.75

title: Question 9: Syntax Errors (MCQ)
codeSnippet:
/*
Answer: C
A is wrong due to single quotes, B is missing a semicolon, D cannot store text in an int.
*/

title: Question 10: New Line Character (MCQ)
description:
What does the \n symbol do when written inside a string in a print statement?

A) Leaves a large space (Tab).
B) Moves to a new line.
C) Deletes the previous word.
D) Prints a comma.
codeSnippet:
/*
Answer: B
It is short for New Line.
*/

title: Question 11: Appropriate Data Types (MCQ)
description:
If we want to store the mathematical value (Pi) 3.14, which type is most appropriate?

A) int
B) double
C) string
D) bool
codeSnippet:
/*
Answer: B
Because it contains a decimal fraction.
*/

title: Question 12: Print Commands (MCQ)
description:
What is the difference between Console.Write and Console.WriteLine?

A) The first prints numbers, the second prints text.
B) The first prints on the screen and stays on the same line, the second prints and moves to a new line.
C) The first receives data and the second prints it.
D) There is no difference.
codeSnippet:
/*
Answer: B
WriteLine is used to move to a new line while Write stays on the same line.
*/

title: Question 13: Concatenation or Addition? (MCQ)
description:
What is the output of this code?

A) The sum is: 7
B) The sum is: 52
C) Error
D) The sum is: a + b

title: Question 14: Case Sensitivity (MCQ)
description:
C# is a Case-Sensitive language. What does that mean?

A) It distinguishes between uppercase (Capital) and lowercase (Small) letters.
B) It does not care about variable names.
C) It is highly sensitive to errors and closes the program immediately.
D) It does not support other languages.
codeSnippet:
/*
Answer: A
The variable 'Age' is entirely different from 'age'.
*/

title: Question 15: Entry Point (MCQ)
description:
The starting point (Entry Point) for executing any program in C# is:

A) class Program
B) using System
C) Main()
D) Start()
codeSnippet:
/*
Answer: C
The Main method is the first method executed by the compiler.
*/

title: Written Question 1: Storing and Printing Data
description: Write a C# code that stores a student's name (like Omar) in one variable, and his age (like 20) in another variable using appropriate data types. Then, print them together on the screen.

title: Written Question 2: Adding Two Numbers
description: Write a C# code that asks the user to input two integer numbers one after the other. The program must read the two numbers, add them together, store the result in a new variable, and finally print the final result on the screen.

title: Written Question 3: Rectangle Area
description: Write a short C# code that asks the user to enter the length and width as integers, then calculates the area of the rectangle (Length * Width) and prints the result.

title: Written Question 4: Data Type Definition
description:
Write 3 lines of code to declare 3 variables and assign them initial values based on the following description:
- A variable to store the first letter of your name.
- A variable to store your weight (might contain decimals).
- A variable indicating whether you have a driver's license or not (true/false).

title: Written Question 5: Predict the Output
description: Trace the following code and write exactly what will be displayed on the screen (Output).
codeSnippet:
/*
Model Answer (Expected Output):
Z = 2
R = 2

Explanation for the student:
Z is 2 because dividing integers ignores fractions (10/4). And R is 2 because the remainder of 10 divided by 4 is 2.
*/
